metadata.title: Operation CuckooBees Transportation Systems Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex Operation CuckooBees aims to disrupt transportation systems by exploiting vulnerabilities in various stages, starting with reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and finally impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets File Target Artifact (Tasks) Application Log File System User Account Credential Store Network Traffic Alternate Protocols
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email Delivery Method Artificial Intelligence File Modification (Mechanism) Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) Scheduled Task/Job Application Log Content (Mechanism) File Metadata (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about transportation systems using publicly available sources.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop custom malware for the attack.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-8193 (Suggested): Citrix ADC, Citrix Gateway, and multiple Citrix SD-WAN WANOP appliance models contain an authorization bypass vulnerability that may allow unauthenticated access to certain URL endpoints. The attacker must have access to the NetScaler IP (NSIP) in order to perform exploitation.
stage.description: The attackers use targeted phishing emails to trick employees into revealing login credentials.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-0028 (Suggested): A Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS URL filtering policy misconfiguration could allow a network-based attacker to conduct reflected and amplified TCP denial-of-service (RDoS) attacks.
stage.description: The attackers compromise employee devices through drive-by downloads.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to maintain persistence on compromised devices.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attackers obfuscate malware code to evade detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt brute-force attacks on authentication protocols.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan the network to identify potential targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers use remote services to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from local systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate collected data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt transportation systems with DoS attacks.

Scenario.summary: Operation CuckooBees uses a complex multi-stage attack plan targeting transportation systems, involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
